Elon shoved 28075 after my 4-bet to 2400. I have 4500 remaining and pot is 30775, giving me ~6.8:1 pot odds. I only need ~13% equity to break even. With AQo, even against a tight range of AA/KK/QQ/AK, I have roughly 25-30% equity. This is a mandatory call - I'm completely pot committed with these odds.

I am UTG with Ace-Jack offsuit in a 6-max game. This is a standard opening hand from any position. I have approximately 50 big blinds, which gives me enough room to raise and evaluate if any of the big stacks behind me (Elon, Minni, or Claude) 3-bet. There are also two short stacks (FiveTwo and Pro) who might shove; AJo is strong enough to open here and potentially call a shove from FiveTwo (14.25 BBs). I will raise to my standard opening size of 300.

With ~14bb and facing an UTG 3bb open, K5o plays poorly: it’s dominated by Flash’s value-heavy opening range (better kings/aces), has weak postflop realization with this stack depth, and isn’t a profitable shove candidate. Calling also risks getting squeezed behind. Best is to preserve chips and wait for a stronger spot.

Facing an UTG open to 300, their range is fairly strong. Ten-Three offsuit is a weak, easily dominated hand and plays poorly multiway and out of position. Calling 300 into a 450 pot gives only ~40% pot odds, but my equity versus an UTG raising range (and with players left to act — button, SB, BB — who can call or 3-bet) is far lower than that. I also don’t want to attempt a light squeeze with this hand; it’s not strong enough to risk a big pot. Fold and wait for a better, more playable spot in position.

Preflop on the button with 3s4d offsuit, a speculative trash hand with minimal playability. Facing an UTG raise from Flash (3x BB), who plays aggressively based on history. Standard fold from any position against early position open, even button, due to wide defender range disadvantage and poor postflop potential. SB and BB still to act with shallow stacks (Pro 2850), no need to get involved. Preserve deep stack for better spots.
T2o is a weak hand facing a UTG raise from Flash. Even getting 250 to win 450 (about 1.8:1), this hand doesn't play well postflop and is dominated too often. Easy fold.

I am in the Big Blind with [7d 9c] facing a raise to 300 (3bb) from Flash in UTG. The pot is 450 and I need to call 200. Although the pot odds are roughly 31%, my hand 97o is weak, especially against a UTG opening range which is typically strong. Furthermore, playing out of position with a marginal hand severely reduces my ability to realize equity. The reverse implied odds and the likelihood of being dominated or bluffed off the hand make this a clear fold. Preserving my 28.5bb stack is more important than defending this specific hand.

6-handed but still first to act with ~14bb, 9♠4♦ offsuit is far too weak to open or limp. Limping invites isolation and puts us in tough spots with a shallow stack; raising/jamming has poor equity and fold equity from this position. Preserve stack for better shove/raise spots.

9♣2♦ is one of the worst starting hands — offsuit, no connectivity and minimal flush/backdoor potential. I’m in middle position with several players left to act (Cutoff Elon, Button Claude, SB Pro, BB Flash), including very deep and aggressive stacks who can easily raise and isolate. Calling 100 to play a multiway pot with virtually no equity is -EV, and opening/raising from my seat with this hand is irresponsible. Preserve the healthy stack and wait for a better spot. Fold.

Preflop, in cutoff after two folds, facing blinds. K3s is a standard open from late position in 6-handed play: suited king with fold equity against blinds, decent playability postflop. SB is short (28bb), BB medium (50bb), button will defend wide. My aggressive image from history supports stealing. Raise to 3x BB (300) to build pot and take initiative. Avoid limping.

J8o is a weak offsuit hand. While I have position on the button, J8o doesn't play well enough to call a cutoff raise. It's too weak to 3-bet as a bluff against Elon who has shown willingness to play big pots. Folding is standard here.

I am in the Small Blind with [3c, Ac] (Ace-Three suited) and a stack of 2800 (28 BB). Elon, the chip leader, has raised to 300 from the Cutoff. While A3s is a decent hand, I am out of position against an aggressive big stack who has shown a willingness to play for stacks (e.g., Hand #63). Flat calling (investing ~9% of my stack) invites a squeeze from the Big Blind and leaves me playing a dominated hand OOP. A 3-bet shove of 28 BB is an overbet and likely only gets called by better hands. A smaller 3-bet commits me too heavily against a chip leader who can easily 4-bet shove. GTO ranges typically fold A3s in the SB vs a CO open at this stack depth to avoid reverse implied odds and difficult post-flop navigation. I choose to preserve my playable stack for a better spot.

I am in the Big Blind with 9-3 suited. Elon has raised to 300 from the Cutoff. While the pot odds are roughly 3.25:1 to call, 9-3 suited is a very weak hand to play out of position against an aggressive chip leader. My stack is currently around 50 big blinds, which is healthy, and there is no need to defend with such a marginal hand and risk being dominated or forced out of the pot on later streets. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I’m UTG in a 6‑handed game with 4♣2♣ — a very weak, low suited hand with poor playability out of position. Limping here just invites raises from several players behind (Elon and Claude have shown aggression and deep stacks) and creates difficult multiway postflop decisions with little equity and bad blocker value. Raising is unjustified — the hand is too weak to open. Even though calling 100 is a small portion of my stack, it’s an unnecessary gamble; better to preserve my 15k and wait for a real opening opportunity. Fold.

Preflop in middle position with 3s9h, an offsuit trash hand with poor playability, low equity, and no speculative value. Facing no raise (just blinds), but folding is standard to avoid difficult postflop spots out of position with a weak range. Pot odds to call 100 into 150 are decent but don't justify playing this hand given multiway potential and my deep stack. Preserve stack for premium spots to maximize long-term EV in the tournament.

Q5o in the cutoff with two folds before me. Marginal hand but decent position for a steal attempt. FiveTwo is short-stacked (1325) which could go either way - might fold to preserve chips or shove. I'll make a standard 2.5x raise to try to take down the blinds. Easy fold if 3-bet.

I hold 2s 4d on the Button, which is one of the weakest starting hands in Texas Hold'em due to its lack of connectivity, rank, and suit coordination. Facing a raise to 250 from Claude in the Cutoff, I have very poor equity against any reasonable opening range. My stack is 28 BB, and calling would be a waste of chips. There is no strategic merit in calling or 3-betting this hand. The correct play is to fold immediately.

I'm holding pocket Jacks in the Small Blind, which is a very strong premium hand. Claude raised from the Cutoff, a position where players typically open with a wide range. To maximize value and prevent the short-stacked Big Blind from seeing a cheap flop, I'll put in a 3-bet. A raise to 850 is a standard sizing from the Small Blind to take initiative and potentially win the pot pre-flop or isolate Claude.

We’re only ~13bb effective and facing a CO open then a SB 3-bet. If we just call 750, we’ll have ~575 behind, creating an SPR so tiny that we’re essentially committed on most flops while playing out of position and potentially letting Claude come along with good odds. A♦T♠ is strong enough to take the high-EV short-stack line here: jam preflop to realize equity, deny Claude the chance to overcall cheaply, and occasionally pick up the dead money when the opener folds. Even when Flash calls (likely given his price), we still have reasonable equity and avoid awkward postflop spots.

I raised Q5o as a steal, got 3-bet by Flash and then 4-bet by FiveTwo who appears to be all-in (stack shows 0). Q5o is a very weak hand to continue with against this action. Even though FiveTwo's all-in is small, I'm likely dominated by both ranges and Flash is still behind me. Easy fold.

I have pocket Jacks, which is a premium starting hand. After I 3-bet Claude's open, FiveTwo went all-in for 1,425 from the Big Blind. Claude has folded, leaving the action to me. I only need to call an additional 575 chips to win a total pot of 2,525. This gives me pot odds of approximately 4.4 to 1. Pocket Jacks is far too strong to fold in this situation, especially against a short-stack shove where my equity is likely very high. Since FiveTwo is all-in and no other players remain, I will call to see the runout.

UTG in 6-handed game with Ks7s. This is a speculative suited hand with decent playability but poor strength from early position. Opening UTG risks playing out of position against the field with a hand that dominates few calling ranges and is vulnerable to domination (e.g., KQs, KJs). Folding is standard to preserve stack and wait for better spots, especially deep-stacked. No limpers ahead, so no incentive to call.
A9o in middle position with Elon folded UTG. This is a reasonable opening hand. I'll make a standard 2.5x raise to 250 to try to win the blinds or isolate.

I am in the Cutoff with Kd 4c facing a raise to 250 from Claude in Middle Position. My stack is 2800 (28 BBs). K4o is a weak hand that is easily dominated by an MP opening range (which includes better Kings and Aces). Calling is unprofitable due to reverse implied odds and being out of position relative to the button. 3-betting is too loose given the hand's poor playability. Folding is the standard and correct play to preserve my stack.

I am on the button with Ace-Queen offsuit, which is a premium starting hand in 6-max play. Claude has opened for a standard raise of 250 from middle position. Given my position and the strength of my hand, a 3-bet is the most effective play to take control of the pot, isolate the raiser, and potentially end the hand pre-flop. With a stack of 3,600 (36 big blinds), I want to apply pressure but maintain some flexibility. A raise to 800 (approximately 3.2x the opening bet) is a standard size that forces the blinds to make a tough decision and puts the initiative back on Claude.

We’re in the SB, out of position, facing an MP open and a BTN 3-bet. 9♠6♦ offsuit has poor playability and equity realization in this spot, is frequently dominated, and calling 750 would commit a big chunk of our 30.5bb stack into a tough multiway/3-bet pot (with the BB still behind). A squeeze/jam is far too loose into ranges that will call. Best is to preserve stack for better spots.

Facing a button 3-bet to 800 over a MP open to 250. Pot is 1200 and I must call 700 from the big blind with Qc5c. Q5s is a weak, often-dominated hand versus a 3-bet range (lots of Qx, broadways, pairs and suited connectors). Calling OOP for 700 requires roughly 37% equity just to break even on the call and will put me in difficult postflop spots, especially if the opener (Claude) remains in. 4-betting as a bluff is also poor — I lack meaningful blockers and won’t get reliable fold equity vs these stacks. Preserve chips for better spots and fold.

Flash 3-bet to 800 from the button. A9o is a marginal hand against a 3-bet range, especially out of position. Flash has only 2800 total, so he's fairly committed. Calling 550 more to play OOP against a pot-committed opponent with A9o isn't ideal. Folding is the disciplined play here - I lose my 250 raise but avoid a potentially costly spot.
T4o from UTG is a trash hand. Easy fold - not playable from early position.

I am in Middle Position with a stack of 28 BBs holding Jack-Four suited (J4s). This hand is disconnected and weak, falling well outside a standard opening range from this position. With aggressive players behind and deep stacks in the blinds, opening this hand is unprofitable. Preserving my stack for better opportunities is the correct play.

I am in the Cutoff position with 9d 6c, which is a very weak hand. Since no one has entered the pot yet, I have the option to open, but 96-offsuit is well below the threshold for an opening range from any position. Folding is the standard and correct play here.

Button vs blinds with ~30bb effective, A2o is strong enough to open as part of a wide stealing range. Both blinds are deep and can defend/3-bet, so I prefer a small raise size that risks less while still applying pressure and building a pot in position. I’m opening with the plan to fold to large 3-bets/jams and play postflop in position when called.

I have 2♥7♠ — essentially the worst starting hand. FiveTwo opened to 225 from the button; I must call 175 more from the small blind and will be out of position postflop with the big blind still to act. Calling requires roughly 31.8% equity (175 into a 550 pot) just to break even preflop; 2-7o has negligible equity vs a typical BTN opening range. A 3-bet bluff would be unnecessary with my healthy stack (15,225) and risks a 4-bet/contested pot from Elon or a call from better hands. Fold and conserve chips for a better spot.

3d4c is a very weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and low card strength. Facing a button raise to 225 (2.25BB), the pot offers 375 with 125 to call (getting ~3:1), but equity against a wide button range is marginal (~30-35%) while being out of position post-flop makes realization difficult. FiveTwo is aggressive but this hand plays poorly multi-street OOP. Standard fold to preserve stack for better opportunities in a long tournament (68/150 hands).

I am holding J6 offsuit in the UTG position, which is one of the weakest starting hands in Texas Hold'em and completely unplayable from early position in a 6-handed game. With 28 big blinds, I have a healthy enough stack to wait for better opportunities and no incentive to bluff or speculate with this trash hand against 5 opponents yet to act. The only correct play is to fold immediately.

I am in Middle Position (MP) with 7s 5s. While suited gappers have some playability, 75s is generally too weak to open from this position in a 6-max game, especially with aggressive players like Elon and Claude still to act behind me. Additionally, there are two short stacks (Pro and FiveTwo) who might look for shove opportunities. Given my current stack of 40BB, I prefer to wait for a stronger opening hand rather than risking chips on a marginal speculative hand.

Unopened pot from the cutoff with J6o at ~32bb. While this can be a marginal open in some 6-max lineups, we’re sandwiched between the button and two large blind stacks who have shown willingness to 3-bet and apply pressure. J6o performs poorly versus continue ranges, and defending/4-betting at this stack depth is awkward. The steal EV doesn’t justify the downside here, so we pass and preserve stack for better spots.

Button vs folded front — wide open. J9o is a fine button-opening hand: playable postflop, decent equity vs blind calling ranges and generates fold equity. I want to take the initiative and steal the blinds or play in position, so I’ll raise rather than limp. A standard 3xBB open (to 300 total) balances aggression and risk — small enough to keep worse hands in, large enough to get folds from the blinds. If I face a large 3-bet from Elon or Claude I’ll re-evaluate and likely fold unless the sizing/reads justify calling or 4-betting; if called I’ll use position to continuation-bet favorable flops and control pot size on adverse boards.

K2o is a very weak offsuit hand with a poor kicker and no suited or connected potential. Facing a button raise from Minni, who often opens wide from late position based on history, but my hand is dominated by most of her continuing range (e.g., any Kx better, pairs, Ax). I'm out of position in the SB, making postflop play difficult even if I flop top pair. Pot odds are about 1.8:1 to call 250 into 450, but implied odds are poor OOP with this hand. No good blockers for 3bet bluff, and my deep stack is better preserved for premium spots. Standard fold to avoid tough situations.

Facing a min-raise from Minni on the button with A6o in the BB. Getting 2.25:1 pot odds (200 to win 450). A6o is a reasonable defend vs a button steal. Minni has been active. Calling is standard here - the hand has some showdown value and playability.

Flopped top pair with A6 on Ad-5c-7c. I also have the 6c for a backdoor flush draw. Top pair weak kicker is a classic check-call hand - I'll let Minni c-bet (which she will do frequently as the preflop aggressor) and then evaluate. No need to lead out and potentially fold out worse hands or bloat the pot OOP with a marginal holding.

I opened from the button and Claude (BB) just called — single opponent and I have position. The flop A♦ 5♣ 7♣ is a relatively dry, low board but contains an overcard (A) that beats me; my J♦9♠ has no pair. Still, this is a good spot to continuation-bet to take the initiative and fold out weaker hands (KQ, small pairs, random floats) or thin value with small pairs. I’ll size small to preserve stack and keep worse hands and floats honest: ~1/3 pot. If Claude folds I win the pot; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and give up to strong aggression or obvious Ax/sets/flushes. If he raises I will usually fold — I have very little equity and no redraws worth getting sticky with given stack depths.

Top pair with A6 on Ad-5c-7c board. Minni's 225 bet into 600 is a small c-bet (~37%). I'm getting excellent odds (225 to win 875). With top pair, backdoor nut flush draw (6c), and a gutshot to the wheel (need a 4), this is a clear call. Raising would only get action from better hands. Check-call and evaluate turn.

Turn brought Jh - a relatively blank card. I still have top pair with weak kicker (A6) on Ad-5c-7c-Jh. I'm out of position and the standard play is to check again. If Minni fires another barrel, I can evaluate sizing and call reasonable bets. No need to lead out and bloat the pot - I'd only get action from better hands (stronger aces, two pair, sets). Check and see what she does.

Turn: Ad 5c 7c Jh — my Jd9s just made top pair with a medium kicker. Heads-up vs Claude, who cold‑called my open and my small flop c‑bet then checked the turn. The board has two clubs so there are live flush draws; checking lets draws see a free river and lets many worse hands check back. I want to both extract value from worse pairs / Jx / Tx and charge club draws, so I’ll bet for value and protection. Size ~50–60% pot is appropriate here: it gets called by worse and prices draws. If Claude raises, his range is likely Ax/sets/very strong hands and I will fold rather than get sticky with a marginal kicker. Action: bet 600 into the 1100 pot; if called I’ll reassess the river; if raised I’ll fold.

Top pair A6 on Ad-5c-7c-Jh. Minni betting 600 into ~1050 (~57% pot). Getting 2.8:1 odds (600 to win 1700). Turn Jh is mostly a blank. She could be value betting worse aces, semi-bluffing club draws, or just barreling. At these odds I need to be good ~26% of the time. Folding top pair to a standard second barrel is too exploitable. Call and evaluate river.

River 9h completes no obvious draws (clubs missed). Minni bet flop and turn, now checked river. This typically indicates: missed draw, giving up with air, or weak made hand. My top pair A6 has decent showdown value but is too thin to value bet - I'd only get called by better aces or two pair+. Check back and take the pot to showdown.

I hold J♦9♠ and the river (Ad 5♣ 7♣ Jh 9h) gives me two pair (Jacks and Nines). Claude cold‑called my open, a small flop c‑bet and my turn bet — passive but sticky line suggesting A‑x, single pairs (5x/7x/xx) or marginal holdings rather than a slow‑played set. He checked the river, which is consistent with pot control or a willingness to call a value bet rather than lead. Checking to showdown surrenders value from Ax, weaker Jx and pocket pairs that will call a reasonable river bet. I want to extract thin value while not overcharging hands that will call; a ~60% pot bet is appropriate. If Claude raises, his range is polarized (better two pair/sets or a bluff) and I’ll fold to sizable raises. Action: value bet 1,400 chips into the 2,300 pot to get called by worse and maximize EV.

River 9h doesn't complete the club flush draw. Minni's sizing (1400 into 3700, ~38% pot) is relatively small. I have top pair with weak kicker. Getting 3.6:1 odds (1400 to win 5100), I need to be good ~27% of the time. Minni could be value betting better aces (AJ, AT, A9) but also has plenty of missed club draws (KcQc, KcTc, etc.) and potential bluffs. Folding top pair at these odds is too exploitable. Call.

I am Under the Gun (UTG) with 98 offsuit. This is a very weak hand to open from early position, especially with a 40BB stack. Standard poker strategy dictates folding such marginal connectors from early position to avoid being out of position against stronger ranges later in the hand.

First-in after UTG folds, Ah7o is a reasonable open in 6-max, especially with 32bb: we block stronger Ax, can win the blinds often, and benefit from having initiative rather than limping into likely isolations from the deep stacks behind. Use a standard raise size to pressure blinds and reduce multiway action; if we face a large 3-bet from the deep stacks, we can comfortably fold.

I have A♠ Q♦ in the cutoff — a strong, standard 3-bet hand. FiveTwo opened to 250 from middle and has a relatively short stack (~29.5bb), so I want to isolate him and take the initiative rather than just calling and letting the button and blinds act behind me. The min-raise to 400 is too small and will invite more callers; I’ll make a standard-sized 3-bet to ~900 total to apply pressure, fold out weaker holdings behind, and retain fold equity. That size is big enough to discourage multiway play but small enough that if FiveTwo shoves I have very playable equity and I still have plenty of chips left if a deeper player (Elon) 4-bets. Plan: 3-bet to 900; fold to a large 4-bet if Elon isolates with heavy action, call FiveTwo’s shove or call small reactions and play postflop with initiative.

AcTd is a premium offsuit ace from the button facing a middle position open and cutoff 3-bet. Excellent position, ace blocker reduces villain's AA/AK range, and good postflop playability. Minni's large 3-bet sizing (900 over 250) polarizes their range to value (QQ+, AK) or bluffs, but FiveTwo is short (~2950 total) and likely to fold. I 4-bet to ~3x the 3-bet (2700 total) to apply pressure, fold out FiveTwo's wide range, and isolate vs Minni if called. SB/BB unlikely to continue without premiums. Deep effective stacks allow flexibility if 5-bet. Folding too tight, calling invites multiway pot OOP players behind.

I have KK - the second best hand in poker. There's been a raise, 3-bet, and 4-bet in front of me. This is a dream scenario for pocket kings. With my stack of 10,975 and needing to call 2,650, if I 5-bet I'm essentially pot-committed anyway. The best play is to shove all-in. I only lose to AA, and given the action, I'm likely ahead of AK, QQ, JJ that could be 4-betting here. Going all-in maximizes value against the range that will call me.

I hold 4h 5s in the Big Blind with a stack of 2700. The action before me is incredibly heavy: an open to 250, a 3-bet to 900, a 4-bet to 2700, and a cold 5-bet jam to over 10,000. My hand is extremely weak relative to the ranges implied by this action (likely QQ+, AK). Committing my entire stack here would be catastrophic given I likely have very little equity against multiple opponents who are showing extreme strength. This is a trivial fold.

We opened A7o and now face an extreme preflop escalation: 3-bet, 4-bet, then a near all-in 5-bet from the SB. With only 2,950 total, calling means committing our entire stack and we’re very likely up against at least one (often two) very strong ranges (QQ+/AK-type), where A7o is dominated and has poor equity. Even with the big pot shown, most of that money is in side-pot territory we can’t win when we’re the short stack; our decision is about the main pot, and A7o doesn’t realize enough equity here. Preserving ~29bb is far higher EV than punting into a likely multiway all-in versus premiums.

I 3‑betted to 900 with A♠Q♦ to isolate the original opener, but the pot just escalated: Elon 4‑bet to 2700 and Claude then shoved to 10975. To call I must put in 10075 more into a 14925 pot — I’d need ~40.3% equity heads‑up (and only ~30.3% if Elon also calls). Claude’s over‑shove over a 4‑bet polarizes to very strong hands (AA/KK/QQ/AK), which dominate AQs; multiway my equity collapses further. Raising all‑in would be unnecessary risk and calling is a marginal/dominated spot given the ranges and pot odds. I’ll stick to the original plan and fold, preserving a deep stack for better spots.

AcTd offsuit is a strong 4-bet hand from the button due to position, ace blocker, and playability, which justified the initial 2700 4-bet to isolate and apply pressure. However, facing Claude's massive 5-bet to 10975 from the SB after folding out the field, his range is extremely strong and polarized: primarily AA, KK, QQ, AKo/s. My Ac blocks AA/AK slightly, but Td provides no strong blockers or straight value, and offsuit reduces flush potential. Calling 8275 into a ~23k pot offers ~2.8:1 odds but poor implied odds postflop OOP against his range (where I'm crushed ~20-30% equity). Effective stacks post-call leave me ~10k behind into 23k pot, vulnerable to further aggression. Raising all-in (18475 total) over-represents and risks stacks vs better, with minimal fold equity left after his sizing suggests commitment. Position advantage insufficient to overcome equity deficit. Folding preserves 18475 stack (15775 effective) for superior spots in deep tournament with 80 hands left. Opponent tendencies from history show Claude capable of big moves with value (e.g., Hand 60, 56), not pure bluffing here.

UTG 6-handed with 7c2h is at the very bottom of the range: terrible playability, dominated when it makes a pair, and no strong suited/connected equity. With ~30bb effective vs most stacks, we don’t want to burn chips limping or opening and getting played back. Best is to preserve stack for better spots.

Unopened pot, I'm in middle position with pocket nines (9s9c) and a deep stack (16,900). This is a clear open-raise candidate — strong medium pair that wants to avoid multiway pots and build value. Standard sizing of ~3x the big blind is appropriate: raise to 300 total (putting in 200 more) to isolate the blinds and get a clearer postflop decision. If called, I'll continue based on flop texture (c-bet dry boards, check/defend on coordinated boards); if I face a short-stack shove I'll call; if I face a large 3-bet from a big stack I'll proceed cautiously and often fold to extreme pressure.

J♠8♣ is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against a middle position raise from Minni, who has been aggressive but likely has a strong range here. Facing a 3x raise preflop in cutoff, standard play is to fold given the multiway potential and better spots available with my deep stack (184 BB). No position advantage justifies defending this marginal hand, and postflop SPR would be awkward. Preserve stack for premium opportunities.

J7s on the button facing a middle position raise. While I have position, J7s is a marginal hand that doesn't play well postflop. Minni has been active but this is still a fold - the hand doesn't have enough playability to justify calling or 3-betting even with position.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Four offsuit (Qs 4h), facing a 3bb open raise from Minni in Middle Position. My stack is 26.5bb. Q4o is a very weak hand that plays poorly, especially out of position against a large stack aggressor. Calling requires putting in roughly 10% of my stack with a dominated hand. There is no strategic merit to calling or raising here. This is a standard fold to preserve chips.

I am in the Big Blind holding Jack-Eight offsuit. Minni opened for 300 (3x) from Middle Position. J8o is a weak hand that does not play well post-flop, particularly out of position against a solid stack like Minni's. With 39 big blinds remaining, I should wait for a better hand or position to commit chips. Folding is the most prudent move here.

UTG in a 6-max game with Q♥3♠ — an off‑suit, poorly connected, easily‑dominated hand. There are five players left to act and stacks are deep, so any limp or open will put me out of position against multiple opponents and make me vulnerable to 3‑bets and tough postflop decisions. Q3o is well outside a standard UTG opening range and has very little playability. No profitable reason to pay 100 or to open; preserve chips for better spots.

Unopened pot after UTG fold (Minni folded). In middle position with Ad8s (A8o), a strong hand with ace blocker, decent playability, and high card strength. Standard to open-raise for initiative, to steal blinds or build pot with position advantage. Opponents have shown aggression historically, but deep stacks favor raising strong hands. Sizing to 3x BB (300) is standard and balanced.
AKo is a premium hand. Facing Elon's raise from MP, I should 3-bet for value. Standard sizing around 3x his raise to 900-950.

I am facing an open raise to 300 from Elon and a 3-bet to 950 from Claude. I hold Queen-Jack offsuit on the Button with a stack of 26.5 BBs. This is a clear fold. My hand is dominated by the 3-bettor's range (AQ, AJ, KQ, pairs), and calling would commit over a third of my stack without closing the action, as the original raiser is still to act. A cold 4-bet shove is also unprofitable as I have little fold equity against the 3-bettor and poor equity when called.

I am in the Small Blind with King-Nine offsuit. There has been an open from Elon and a 3-bet from Claude. K9o is too weak to call or 4-bet given the action and my position. Calling would commit a significant portion of my stack (900 out of 3850) out of position with a hand that is likely dominated by the 3-betting range. Therefore, folding is the best play.

Facing an MP open (Elon) and a CO 3-bet (Claude) with Elon still to act behind me, calling KTh becomes a high reverse-implied-odds spot: when I make top pair I’m often dominated by AK/KQ, and my stack-to-pot ratio after calling (pot ~2250, ~2000 behind) leaves little room to maneuver postflop OOP. A jam risks running into a strong calling range that won’t fold often given the price, so fold equity is unlikely to be high enough. Best play is to preserve chips and wait for a cleaner spot.

Preflop, UTG folded, I opened to 300 from MP with Ad8o, a decent speculative hand but offsuit. Claude 3-bets to 950 from CO, a strong position. Blinds and button folded, so heads-up. My hand has an ace blocker but poor playability OOP vs a 3-bet range which likely includes QQ+, AKo/AJs+, and some bluffs. Pot odds are ~2.15:1 (1400 pot, 650 to call), requiring ~32% equity to call profitably immediately, and A8o has decent equity (~35-40% vs polarized range), but OOP postflop dynamics are tough: vulnerable to continuation bets, hard to realize equity on most boards. 4-betting as a bluff with ace blocker is possible (min to 1600 total, or larger like 2500), but Claude's stack (14k) and history of aggression (frequent 3-bets, 4-bets like H56, H70) suggest he can continue wide. Folding preserves my deep 181bb stack (18175) for better spots in a 6-handed tournament with 78 hands left; variance control maximizes long-term EV. Tighten up post-3bet OOP.

A9o is a playable hand UTG in 6-handed play, within the top ~15-20% range. Deep stack, no action yet, position allows stealing blinds and building pot OOP with ace blocker. Standard open raise size of 3x BB to 300 given pot of 150 and history of similar sizing. Avoid limping OOP.

4h5s is a weak hand - unsuited low cards with poor playability. Facing an UTG raise of 300, this is a clear fold. The hand lacks both high card value and suited connectivity. No reason to invest chips here.

I am holding Jack-Five offsuit in the Cutoff facing a raise to 300 (3BB) from Elon in the Under-The-Gun (UTG) position. An UTG opening range is typically strong, consisting of pocket pairs, strong aces, and broadway cards. J5o has very poor equity against this range and limited playability post-flop. With a stack of roughly 26.5BB, I cannot afford to call speculative raises with trash hands. Folding is the only standard and profitable play here.

I am on the button with Ten-Eight offsuit. Elon, who has a very large stack, has raised to 3x from UTG. Ten-Eight offsuit is a weak hand that is likely dominated by Elon's UTG opening range (which typically includes better tens, high cards, and pairs). While I have the advantage of position, my hand does not play well against a strong range. Given my current stack of 38.5 BB, there is no need to defend with such a marginal hand or attempt a high-risk bluff against an early position opener. Folding is the most disciplined and standard play here.

Elon opened UTG to 3bb, which is typically a relatively strong range at 6-max. Q5o plays very poorly out of position from the small blind, and with 28bb we don’t have a profitable 3-bet (too weak to value, too dominated to bluff effectively) or a good call (reverse implied odds, BB can come along with position and a big stack). Best is to preserve stack and wait for better spots.

Elon opened to 300 UTG in a 6‑hand game — a fairly strong, value-heavy range. I’m in the big blind with K♠3♣ (K3o), which is heavily dominated by his Kx and broadway combos and plays very poorly out of position. Calling 200 more to win a 450 pot gives only ~31% equity needed; K3o’s equity vs an UTG opening range is around or below that and will be tough to realize OOP vs an aggressive, deep stack. A light reraise is a thin bluff that’s unlikely to fold out a reasonable UTG range and turns a marginal spot into a bloated pot. With deep stacks and plenty of better spots to pick from, the +EV choice is to fold and preserve my stack.

K6o is a weak hand to open from UTG. Even in a 6-handed game, this is outside my opening range from early position. I have a healthy stack at 154 BBs, no need to take marginal spots from the worst position. Folding is standard here.

I hold Q7 offsuit in Middle Position (HJ) with 26.5 BBs. This is a statistically weak hand that is easily dominated. With four players left to act, including two massive stacks in the blinds, opening this hand carries negative expected value. Preserving my stack for better opportunities is the correct play.

I am in the Cutoff with Queen-Four offsuit (Q4o). This hand is significantly below the threshold for an opening range in this position. With a stack of 3850 (38.5 BB), there is no strategic reason to attempt a steal or enter the pot with such a weak hand, especially with three players yet to act behind me. Folding is the standard and correct play.

Folds to us on the button with blinds in the pot. K6o is too weak to limp/call big pots but has decent blocker value (K) and plays well enough in position to profitably steal versus two opponents who will have to defend out of position. With 28bb, I prefer a small open to keep risk down and allow a clean fold if we face a strong 3-bet.

J♣8♣ is a playable suited connector but I'm out of position versus the button. FiveTwo on the button is a mid-short stack likely opening a wide stealing range to 225. A light 3-bet OOP here is suboptimal: the minimum 3-bet (to 350) is small and invites squeezes from the big blind (Elon) or a caller from the button; I don't want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand. Calling 175 more (225 total committed) gives good pot odds to see a flop, preserves stack depth, and lets me realise equity with a suited J and backdoor straight/flush potential. Plan postflop: proceed cautiously out of position — fold to heavy action unless I pair or hit strong draws; otherwise try to realise equity or take free cards when checked to.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 2.25BB and a small blind call. My hand 8h5d is offsuit, disconnected, and has poor playability out of position postflop against two opponents. While pot odds are decent (calling 125 into 550, ~4.4:1), the hand has low equity against typical button opening ranges (dominated often by better broadways, pairs, suited connectors) and the SB calling range. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities in a deep-stack tournament phase (182 BB effective), avoiding speculative OOP multiway pots with trash.

I called preflop to see a flop with Jc8c, but on Ad-6h-5c I have no pair and only a very weak backdoor club (needs two clubs). The board heavily favors the button's opening range (many Ax and A-high combos) and I'm out of position. Bluffing here has low fold equity and will often be called by A's or middle pairs, so I should control the pot and realize equity. Plan: check and fold to meaningful pressure unless I pick up a pair or a real draw on the turn; if the button checks back I can see a free turn and re-evaluate.

Heads-up in position after opening BTN and getting called by SB. On A65r we have second pair with a decent kicker plus a backdoor club draw. This is a strong range-advantage board for the preflop raiser, and Minni’s check contains a lot of unpaired hands that will fold to a small c-bet. Betting small also denies equity to two-overcard floats while keeping the pot controlled with our medium-strength made hand. If we face a check-raise we can comfortably fold given our hand class and stack depth.

Board: Ad-6h-5c. I hold Jc8c — no pair, only a weak backdoor club and slim turn/improvement outs. FiveTwo (button) opened wide preflop and now c-bets small (200 into a 750 pot), a size that contains many A-high/air hands as well as value A-x — but it gives me ~21% pot odds to call (200 to win ~950). Out of position and unwilling to bloat the pot with a marginal hand, but calling cheaply to realize equity and see a turn card is the best path. Plan: call the 200 to see the turn; if I pick up a pair, a club, or significant equity I can proceed, but fold to heavy turn/river pressure if I don’t improve. Do not attempt a bluff-raise OOP here.

Turn is Ad-6h-5c-3s and I hold Jc8c. My backdoor club possibility from the flop is dead now (turn is a spade), so I have no meaningful draw — only outs are 3 jacks + 3 eights = 6 outs (~13% to pair on the river). Pot is 950 and there is currently no bet. Betting out OOP with a marginal J-high gives very little fold equity against the button’s wide but A-heavy/second-pair-capturing range and just bloats the pot when I have poor equity. I should control the pot and see the river for free. Plan: check and fold to significant river aggression unless I pair (then reassess/call small bets or proceed cautiously vs raises).

Turn check from SB after calling flop on A65 is a range that’s already fairly strong (a lot of Ax, some 6x/5x, and now some 74/42 type straights/two-pair that improved on the 3). With K6 we have decent showdown value but we don’t get much value from worse hands with a second barrel, and we risk getting check-raised off our equity or bloating a pot where Ax dominates us. In position with SPR ~2.5, the best line is pot-control, take the free river, and evaluate versus a river bet sizing (call small, fold to big pressure).

Board: Ad 6h 5c 3s Kd. I hold Jc8c — I never improved: no pair, no club flush, river brick. I checked turn intending to see a free river and fold to aggression unless I paired; that remains the correct plan. FiveTwo opened wide and c-bet the flop small, then checked the turn — his range still contains many A-x, K-x and some medium/slowplayed made hands that all beat J-high. Betting OOP as a bluff here has very little fold equity and only bloats the pot with essentially zero showdown value. With the pot at 950 and FiveTwo having ~2375 behind, any river value bet from him will put me in a losing, low-equity spot. Action: check and fold to value bets — don’t bluff or bloat the pot with J-high.

River improves us to two pair (K6) on A-6-5-3-K. After Minni check-calls flop and then checks turn and river, her range is heavy on bluff-catchers (a lot of Ax, some 6x/5x, and occasional Kx) that can reasonably call a small value bet. The hands that beat us (A6/A5/AK, sets like 55/33, and the wheel straight 42) are a small part of the range but will often check-raise, so we want a bet size that gets paid by worse while keeping our bet/fold clean versus a big raise from a deep stack. I’ll go for a small-to-medium value bet to target one-pair aces and worse two-pair like K5/K3/65.

I stick to my original plan and fold. I never improved with J♣8♣ — the river K♦ is a brick for my hand and my backdoor club draw was killed on the turn. FiveTwo’s line (wide open preflop, small flop c-bet, check on turn, small river bet of 350) is exactly the sort of line that contains a lot of A‑x and K‑x (thin value) plus a few bluffs, but it doesn’t give me reliable show‑down equity. The math is clear: pot is 1300 and the call is 350, so I’d need roughly 21% equity (350/(1300+350) ≈ 21%). With no pair and no further streets to improve, my hand only beats rare pure bluffs — my true equity vs his likely range is well below the required threshold. Raising as a bluff OOP with J‑high would bloat the pot unnecessarily and is a poor use of my stack. Preserve chips for better spots — fold.

I am UTG with Qh 9s, an off-suit gapped connector. With 26.5 BBs, I need to be disciplined with my opening range, especially from early position. Q9o is easily dominated by hands that call or 3-bet me (AQ, KQ, QJ, pairs). Given the aggressive table dynamics observed in previous hands, opening this weak hand out of position is -EV. The standard and correct play is to fold.

I am holding Ten-Five offsuit in middle position. This is a very weak starting hand and does not warrant a raise or a call, especially with four players still to act behind me, including several large stacks and active players. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Unopened pot in the cutoff with ~33bb. A7o is strong enough to open in 6-max from CO, leveraging position and fold equity versus the blinds. Raising (rather than limping) keeps initiative, can take it down pre, and plays better postflop with range advantage. Use a standard small open to pressure blinds without bloating the pot too much versus deeper stacks behind.

Button with A♠J♠ facing a 250 cutoff open — very playable hand in position. I want to 3-bet to isolate the opener, take initiative, and gain fold equity against weaker broadway and suited hands. A sizing of ~3.5x the open (raise to 900 total) is large enough to discourage multiway action and thin out the field, but small enough relative to stacks to keep postflop play comfortable. If FiveTwo shoves I can call (decent equity vs a wide raising range); if the blinds 3-bet I will re-evaluate based on sizing and opponent. Plan: c-bet favorable flops and use position to extract value or fold if heavily resisted.

Q8o is a weak offsuit hand with limited playability, especially out of position in the small blind. Facing a cutoff open-raise to 250 and a button 3-bet to 900, the ranges of FiveTwo and Minni are strong (value-heavy for Minni's 3-bet, polarized possibly). Calling 850 into a pot of 1300 would give pot odds of ~28% to win at showdown, but equity against their combined ranges is insufficient, and playing OOP post-flop with this hand is disastrous due to poor implied odds and reverse implied odds if I hit top pair. No 4-bet bluff value here with blockers or nut potential. Deep stacks don't help this marginal spot. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities.
4d 9c is a very weak offsuit hand with no connectivity. Facing a 3-bet from Minni (who has been active), I have terrible equity against any reasonable 3-betting range. Easy fold.

Minni’s 3-bet to 900 vs our 250 open puts us in a tough spot with A7o: we’ll be out of position postflop, often dominated when we make top pair, and calling creates a very low SPR (~1.1) where we’re likely forced into high-variance decisions with a marginal hand. A 4-bet jam has limited fold equity (she’s getting a good price to call) and A7o performs poorly versus her continuing range. Preserving our ~30bb stack is higher EV than taking a dominated, realization-poor defend.

I am sitting UTG (Under The Gun) in a 6-max game with Ace-Seven offsuit. While an Ace has some value, A7o is generally too weak to open from this position as it is easily dominated by the ranges of the players behind me. Given the stack sizes of the remaining players and the aggression shown in previous hands by Elon and Claude, folding is the most disciplined and standard play here to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

We’re first in from MP with QQ at ~31bb effective. This is a premium that wants to build the pot and deny equity to overcards; limping invites multiway action from several aggressive stacks behind (Minni/Elon/Claude) and puts us in tougher spots. Standard open size at 50/100 is ~2.5–3x; 300 pressures the blinds and sets up a clean stack-to-pot ratio. If we face a 3-bet from late position, QQ is strong enough to continue aggressively (often by 4-bet jamming given our stack depth).

Cutoff with A♣T♠ facing a 3BB open to 300 from middle — a hand I want to play for value and often isolate the opener. I'll 3-bet to 1000 total: big enough to apply pressure to FiveTwo (≈27BB) and discourage the blinds from coming along for a multiway pot, but small enough to keep options vs the button. Plan: take the pot down preflop or play HU postflop; if I face a large 4-bet/shove (clear polarization) I'll fold A‑T offsuit since it is frequently dominated. If I get a flat call from the button or opener, proceed cautiously postflop, c-betting favorable textures and re-evaluating to avoid bloating a pot OOP with a marginal kicker.

K2o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability and dominated by any king in opponents' ranges. Facing an open from FiveTwo (short stack at 2775) and a 3-bet to 1000 from aggressive Minni (deep stack), the 3-bet range is strong (e.g., pairs, AK, AQ+). Pot odds require ~41% equity to call 1000 into 1450, but K2o has far less vs that range, especially postflop. No reason to speculate on button here; preserve stack for better spots.
85o is a very weak hand facing a 3-bet. Even with ~1.5:1 pot odds, this hand has no high card value, isn't suited, and plays poorly postflop. Easy fold.

I hold Kh 2c in the Big Blind with a stack of 25.5 BB. Facing a raise from Middle Position and a large 3-bet from the Cutoff, my hand is heavily dominated by both opponents' ranges. K2o has very poor equity against a 3-betting range, and I would be committing a significant portion of my stack to call or shove. Preserving my stack for a better spot is the clear choice.

We opened QQ and face a cutoff 3-bet to 1000 with everyone behind already folded. With ~27.8bb total and 2475 behind after our open, flat-calling creates an awkward SPR and invites tough postflop spots where overcards can realize equity; we also don’t want to 4-bet small and leave a tiny, committed stack. Shoving maximizes value and denial versus hands like JJ/TT/AQ/AK while still giving fold equity against the top of Minni’s light 3-bet range. If called, QQ is comfortably ahead of most calling ranges except KK/AA and is fine versus AK.

I 3-bet to 1,000 to isolate the opener, but FiveTwo reraised to 2,775 (effectively a large 4‑bet/all‑in). Calling 1,775 more would require ~31% equity to break even (pot ~5,700). A♣T♠ is frequently dominated by a polarized 4‑bet range (AA/KK/QQ/JJ/AK and strong Axs) and does not have the equity to call or the reason to 5‑bet here out of position. My preflop plan was to fold A‑T offsuit to a clear 4‑bet/shove, so I fold and preserve a healthy stack for better spots.

Js6c offsuit is too weak to open from UTG in 6-max, especially with several deeper stacks behind who can 3-bet and put us in dominated, reverse-implied-odds spots. Limping also performs poorly and invites multiway action where our top pairs are second-best too often. Preserve the 42bb stack and wait for better UTG holdings.

Ten-three of clubs in middle position is a weak, often dominated hand with poor playability. The pot is just the blinds (150) and calling 100 to see a flop is a marginal speculative call with several players yet to act (Cutoff, Button, SB, BB) who can apply pressure. With a healthy stack I should be selective and wait for a better spot or position — folding preserves chips for profitable opportunities. Fold.

Preflop in cutoff with 2h9s offsuit, a very weak hand lacking connectivity, high cards, or suitedness. Standard opening range from CO does not include 29o. While folded to me, stealing blinds risks facing a 3-bet from deep-stacked button Claude or a shove from short-stacked SB Pro (25BB) or BB Flash (37.5BB), and postflop playability is poor. Better to fold and wait for a stronger spot to build the stack.
Button with A3o after everyone folds to me. This is a standard steal opportunity against just the blinds. A3o is strong enough to raise for value/steal in this spot with position. Standard 2.5x open.

I am in the Small Blind with 8d5d, facing a 2.5x raise from the Button (Claude). My stack is 25 BBs. While 85s has some playability, defending from the Small Blind against a 2.5x open is generally a losing play, especially out of position and with the Big Blind still to act. The pot odds (requiring ~33% equity realization) are not compelling enough given the positional disadvantage and the risk of being squeezed by the BB. At this stack depth, preserving chips for higher equity spots or shove/fold scenarios is preferable.

I have a pocket pair of fives in the Big Blind. Claude raised from the Button, which is often a wide range. I am getting good pot odds (2.67:1) to call and see a flop. With my stack size of 37.5 BB, I can afford to call and set-mine or evaluate post-flop. 3-betting is an option but calling is lower variance and allows me to play a pot with a hand that has good post-flop potential.

I have flopped a set of fives on an Ace-high board with a potential flush draw. As I am out of position against the pre-flop aggressor (Claude on the Button), the standard play is to check to the raiser to allow them to make a continuation bet. This conceals the strength of my hand and allows me to extract value from Claude's bluffs or weaker holdings like top pair (Ax). I am looking to build the pot significantly on later streets or check-raise if the sizing is appropriate, but for now, checking is the most effective way to disguise my monster hand.
Flopped top pair with Ac on 5c Ah 8c. Flash checked to me. Good spot for a c-bet - I have top pair and the Ac blocks the nut flush draw. Betting around 60% pot for value and to charge draws.

I have flopped bottom set (555) on an Ace-high board with a flush draw. This is a monster hand in this situation. Since Claude, the pre-flop aggressor, is likely to continue with a wide range including any Ace or flush draw, I want to build the pot immediately to maximize value. A check-raise to 1000 (roughly 3x his bet) allows me to extract value from his strong one-pair hands and draws while setting up the pot for a potential shove on the turn or river.

Flash check-raised my c-bet from 325 to 1000 on A-high board with flush draw possible. I have top pair with weak kicker but crucially hold the Ac blocking the nut flush draw. Flash has only ~1600 behind after this bet, so we're getting close to committed. Folding top pair with nut flush blocker seems too tight. Raising would commit me with a marginal hand. Calling keeps the pot manageable, lets me see the turn with position, and I can re-evaluate. If a club comes I have the blocker, if the board pairs I can fold to aggression.

The 9c is an extremely coordinated card that completes the club flush and several straight draws (67, 7T, J7). Having check-raised the flop and been called by Claude, I must respect the possibility that his calling range included flush draws or an Ace with a high club. My set of fives has significantly decreased in relative strength. By checking, I am looking to control the pot size and see how Claude responds. If Claude checks back, I can evaluate the river; if he bets, I will have to decide based on his sizing whether my hand still functions as a viable bluff-catcher or if I am beaten by the completed draws.

Flash check-raised flop aggressively then checked when the third club (9c) hit. This is a peculiar line. With the Ac in my hand, I block the nut flush. Flash checking the turn after the flush completes could mean: (1) they had a strong hand scared of the flush, (2) they're trapping with a made flush, or (3) they had a draw that's now giving up. With only 2600 behind and pot at 2550, any significant bet commits me. My top pair weak kicker with nut flush blocker has showdown value but isn't strong enough to bet/call a check-raise. Checking back controls the pot, gets to showdown against busted draws, and avoids getting trapped by a slowplayed flush.

The board has developed in a way that is disastrous for my set of fives. With four clubs now on the board, any single club in Claude's range (which is wide as he raised from the Button) beats me. There is also a straight possibility (6x). I cannot bet for value because no hand worse than mine will call, and I cannot effectively bluff given my stack-to-pot ratio and the likelihood that Claude holds at least a low club or an Ace with a club kicker. Checking is the only viable option to reach showdown as cheaply as possible. If Claude bets, I will likely have to fold, as my set has been reduced to a weak bluff-catcher on this extremely coordinated board.

I have the nut flush with the Ac on a 4-club board. Flash check-raised flop then checked turn and river. With the nuts, I need to extract value. Flash has 2600 behind and the pot is 2550. I'll bet around 1600 to get called by any worse flush or strong hands that might hero call.

The board has become extremely dangerous for my set of fives. With four clubs now on the board, any single club in Claude's range beats me. Additionally, the straight (6x or Tx) is now possible. Claude's bet of 1600 into the 2550 pot is significant, especially considering my remaining stack of 2600. While Claude's range as a button raiser is wide, his call of my check-raise on the flop suggests he has a piece of the board or a draw, many of which would have contained a club (like AcX, KcX, or suited connectors). Since my set has been reduced to a weak bluff-catcher that loses to any club, any 6, and any 10, it is highly likely I am beaten. Hero-calling here would leave me with only 10BB if I lose, so preserving my 26BB stack is the better tournament strategy.

UTG in a 6-max game I need to play a fairly tight opening range. Q6o is a weak, poorly connected, dominated hand and performs badly out of position — especially with aggressive, deep stacks (Elon/Claude) and medium stacks (Button/SB) who can 3-bet or call and create a multiway pot. Calling 100 to play OOP or opening with this hand invites pressure and tough postflop decisions; standard UTG ranges don’t include Q6o. Preserve my 15.9k stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

9s2d is a complete trash hand: offsuit, disconnected, low cards with minimal playability or straight potential. In middle position pre-flop with 182BB effective stack in a 6-handed tournament, there's no reason to limp or raise this speculative hand. Folding preserves chips for better spots. Opponents have shown aggression historically, but no need to enter with this.

Pocket 5s in the cutoff with two folds in front. This is a standard open-raise situation. I have position and a decent pocket pair that plays well multiway or heads up. Opening to 2.5x (250) is standard. The stacks behind are relatively short which is something to consider if I face a 3-bet, but 55 is definitely strong enough to open here.

I am holding Ten-Five offsuit (T5o) on the Button. Claude has raised to 250 from the Cutoff. With a stack of 25 BBs, I cannot afford to bleed chips calling with trash hands. T5o is not a suitable candidate for a 3-bet bluff or a cold call. The disciplined and correct play is to fold immediately.

I am in the Small Blind with Ten of Clubs and Four of Hearts, which is a very weak hand. Claude, a deep-stacked player, has raised to 250 from the Cutoff. My stack is relatively short (25.5 BBs). Calling with such a marginal hand out of position would be a negative EV play, as I would likely be forced to fold on many flops or lose more chips trying to navigate post-flop with a weak range. Given the strength of my opponents and my current stack size, I should wait for better opportunities.

Claude is opening from the cutoff, which should be a fairly wide range 6-handed. Getting a good price in the big blind (150 more to win a pot that will be 550), A3o has enough raw equity to defend, and it plays reasonably with top-pair potential plus wheel/straight possibilities. 3-betting is possible as a blocker bluff, but with our stack and being out of position versus a deep opponent, calling keeps the pot controlled and realizes our equity without inflating variance.

We defend pre and flop comes J♠7♣3♣ giving us bottom pair (3s) with an ace kicker but no club. This is not a strong enough hand to build a pot out of position with a donk bet, and leading also isolates us against better (Jx/overpairs/strong draws) while folding out worse hands we’d like to keep in. Standard and highest-EV is to check our range to the preflop raiser, then evaluate vs Claude’s c-bet sizing (often continue vs small bets, tighten vs large pressure).

I have pocket 5s on J73 with two clubs. Heads up against BB who called. This is a dry-ish board where I have an underpair. I should c-bet small for value against worse pairs (22-44) and to deny equity from overcards. FiveTwo's BB calling range missed this board often. A 1/3 pot c-bet is standard.

Claude’s flop c-bet is very small (175 into 725), which gives us an excellent price to continue. With A3 on J♠7♣3♣ we have a pair, an ace kicker (important vs some random floats), and we can improve to two pair/trips. Raising would bloat the pot OOP and mostly gets action from better (Jx/overpairs/strong club draws) while folding out a lot of worse hands we dominate/keep in, so calling is the highest-EV, lowest-variance line and keeps Claude’s range wide for later streets.

Turn pairs the J and completes the third club, so ranges polarize a bit: Claude can have strong hands (Jx now trips, some two-club flushes) but also a lot of flop c-bets that want to take a free card/check back (A-high, pocket pairs without a club, 7x, single-club floats). With A♠3♦ we’ve improved to two pair (Jacks and treys) — good enough to value/protect, but not strong enough to happily face a big raise on this texture.

Leading small accomplishes three things: (1) gets thin value from 7x/pocket pairs/A7/single-club hands that will often check back, (2) sets a reasonable price so we don’t face an oversized turn probe, and (3) gives us a clean fold vs a raise, which will be heavily weighted to flushes/trips on this board. A ~1/3 pot sizing keeps the pot controlled with our medium-strength made hand OOP.

Turn brings Jc - pairing the board and completing the flush draw. FiveTwo leads small (300 into 850). My 55 is now in trouble - I lose to any jack, any flush, 77, or 33. However, the small bet sizing could indicate a probe/block bet rather than monster strength. With pot odds of 300 to win 1500 (20% needed), I can call once to see a river. If I face significant aggression on the river, I'll fold. No reason to raise here as I'm only getting called by better.

River brings the 4th club, meaning any single club in Claude’s hand now makes a flush, and this line (flop small c-bet, call; call our turn lead on J♣) contains a lot of one-club continues. Our hand (A♠3♦) is still just two pair (Jacks and treys) — not a full house and no club to improve — so betting is very thin and mostly gets action from better (flushes/Jx/full houses) while folding out the worse hands (7x/pairs/no-club floats) that we beat.

Best play is to check and treat this as a bluff-catcher: we keep the pot from inflating when behind, and we allow Claude to bet missed/no-club hands sometimes. We can then respond based on sizing/read, but leading here is low EV.

River completes the 4-flush with all clubs on board (Js 7c 3c Jc 4c). I have 55 with no club - essentially air. FiveTwo checked, likely either giving up without a club or trapping. There's zero value in betting as I only get called by better hands (any club, any jack). Check back for a free showdown and hope they have nothing.

Pocket 8s is a premium hand from UTG in a 6-handed game. Standard play is to raise for value and to isolate against the blinds. No pre-flop action yet, pot has blinds (150 total). Raising to 4x the BB (400) builds the pot while maintaining fold equity against short stacks like Pro (2500) and Flash (2550). My deep stack (18k) supports this, and history shows opponents like Minni and Claude defend wide but fold to pressure often. Avoids multiway pot OOP.
K6o is a weak hand facing an UTG raise. Out of position with a dominated king and poor kicker. Easy fold - not profitable to call here.

I hold Kc 3h in the Cutoff facing a 4x raise (400 chips) from Elon in UTG. K3o is a very weak hand that plays poorly post-flop and is dominated by the strong range of a UTG opener. My stack is 2500 (25 BB), and calling 400 represents a significant portion (16%) of my stack, which is incorrect for such a speculative and weak hand. With players left to act behind me and a strong open in front, the only standard play is to fold and preserve my stack for a better opportunity.

I am holding 9-2 offsuit, which is arguably the weakest hand in poker. Elon has raised to 400 from under-the-gun, suggesting a strong opening range. My stack is relatively short at 25.5 big blinds, and committing nearly 16% of it to see a flop with such poor equity would be a massive strategic error. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

UTG opened large (4bb) from early position, which heavily weights Elon toward stronger holdings. J2s is very dominated when called and plays poorly out of position, and if we flat the 350 more we also invite the BB (deep stacked) to squeeze, putting us in a tough spot. With ~34.5bb effective, this is not a profitable defend versus this sizing/position, so we conserve chips and fold.

Elon opened UTG to 400 (4x). I’m in the BB with T♠7♣ — a weak, offsuit hand that plays poorly out of position. Pot is 550 and it costs me 300 to call, which requires ~35% equity; T7o is often dominated by an UTG opener and won’t have that equity or much post‑flop maneuvering room OOP. 3‑betting as a bluff is thin (I don’t have good blockers and would be out of position against a likely stronger range), and preserving a healthy 15,825 stack is the priority. Fold and wait for a better spot.
J7o is a weak hand from UTG position. Not suited, poor connectivity, and I have 5 players behind me. This is a clear fold from early position.

I hold Ace-Jack offsuit (As Jc) in Middle Position with 25 big blinds (2500 chips). The player under the gun has folded. AJo is a strong broadway hand suitable for opening from this position in a 6-handed game. My stack depth allows for a standard open raise rather than a shove. I will raise to 2.5x the big blind (250 chips) to build the pot and thin the field, aiming to take down the blinds or play the hand in position against the blinds.

I am holding 9-6 offsuit in the Cutoff position. Middle position (Pro) has opened with a raise to 250. 9-6 offsuit is a very weak hand and does not play well against a standard opening range. Given my stack size of approximately 25.5 big blinds, it is important to conserve chips for more favorable opportunities rather than calling raises with marginal or weak hands. Fold is the most disciplined and correct play.

Facing a 2.5x open from a shortish stack (Pro ~22.5bb), Th2o is too weak to continue. Calling invites squeezes from the blinds and plays poorly postflop even in position, while 3-betting risks getting jammed on and having to fold after investing extra chips. Best EV is to preserve chips and wait for a better spot.

4♠2♥ is essentially unplayable. I’m out of position (SB) facing a standard MP open to 250 with 200 to call. Calling OOP with such trash leads to very poor equity and terrible postflop decisions; three-betting as a bluff is too thin (and risks a shove from the short 2,250-stack opener or a call from the BB). Stack is healthy enough to wait for a much better spot. Fold and preserve chips.

Pocket Queens is an extremely strong premium hand, among the best starting hands in Hold'em. Pro's raise to 250 from middle position is standard but likely includes a wide range including pairs, broadway, suited connectors, etc., much of which we dominate. As BB OOP, calling keeps the pot multiway potentially and gives up initiative, but 3-betting isolates, builds the pot for value, and applies pressure on Pro's ~22BB stack. A 3-bet to 800 (roughly 3.2x their raise) is a standard sizing here—large enough to fold out trash but small enough to get calls from worse like TT-JJ, AK, AQ. If Pro shoves, QQ is often ahead and we have fold equity against lighter calls. Deep stack allows flexibility post-flop if called. Folding or flat-calling is suboptimal.

I opened to 250 from Middle Position with AJo and faced a 3-bet to 800 from the Big Blind (Elon). This is a large 3-bet (3.2x my open), and my remaining stack is 2250 (starting the hand with 2500, ~25BB). Calling 550 would require committing roughly 32% of my starting stack pre-flop with a hand that is often dominated by a 3-betting range (AK, AQ, JJ+). AJo plays poorly in bloated pots even in position, as I likely won't realize my equity if I miss the flop. Shoving 25BB is too loose as I have little fold equity and am behind the calling range. Preserving my 22.5BB stack is the best strategic option in this spot.

I am in UTG position with a stack of 22.5 BBs (2250 chips). I hold As6s. In a 6-handed game, A6 suited is a marginal opening hand from early position. With the shortest stack at the table and aggressive big stacks behind me (Elon, Claude, Minni), opening puts me at risk of being 3-bet or playing out of position dominated. Standard charts for this stack depth often recommend folding A6s-A9s from UTG to preserve fold equity for stronger hands or better positions. Therefore, I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I am in Middle Position with King-Seven offsuit and a stack of 2550 (25.5 BB). This hand is too weak to open-raise from this position, especially given the aggressive play seen from the larger stacks (Elon, Claude, Minni) and the potential for a 3-bet from the remaining players. Conserving my stack for a better starting hand is the optimal strategy here.

Folds to us in the cutoff with only blinds in the pot. K2 offsuit is too dominated to open profitably versus three players behind (button + blinds), especially with capable/aggressive opponents who can 3-bet or defend wide, putting our 34.5bb stack in awkward postflop spots. No reason to limp; we’ll pass and wait for a better steal/iso candidate.

Button vs two blinds with Q♦6♣ — a hand worth opening but not committing a lot. I want to leverage position and fold equity: make a standard 3xBB open to 300 to steal the blinds or play a single-raised pot in position. Size is large enough to discourage some light calls/plays but small relative to both stacks. Plan: collect the blinds if both fold; if one calls play postflop for pot control and positional advantage; if I face a sizable 3-bet (much larger than a standard isolation) I will fold this marginal hand and preserve stack. This balances aggression with caution given table tendencies and deep stacks.

KsQs is a premium suited broadway hand, strong for 3-betting out of the SB against a button open range, which is typically wide. Minni's history shows aggression from late position, but KQs has excellent equity, playability, and fold equity. 3-betting isolates, builds the pot, and pressures lighter holdings. Sizing to 900 (3x the open) is standard with deep stacks (18k+ effective vs Minni). Avoids multiway pot OOP by potentially folding out BB. Calling risks poor postflop position multiway; folding too tight.
5c8d is a weak offsuit hand facing a 3-bet from Elon. Poor playability out of position, not worth calling 800 to defend. Easy fold.

I opened to 300 from the button with Q♦6♣ as planned, but Elon’s raise to 900 is a sizable 3‑bet and is likely weighted toward hands that dominate or heavily favorite against Q6o (AQ+, KQ, mid/high pairs, broadways). Calling 600 into a 1,300 pot requires roughly ~32% equity — Q6 offsuit rarely has that versus a typical SB 3‑bet range and I don’t have a reliable read that Elon is wide enough to make this +EV. I also won’t 4‑bet bluff with this hand. Follow my preflop plan and preserve a healthy stack for better, more profitable spots in position — fold here.

I am in the Under the Gun (UTG) position with 4h 2d, which is one of the weakest hands in Texas Hold'em. Given the position and the hand's lack of playability and equity, there is no strategic reason to enter the pot. Folding is the only viable play here to conserve chips for better opportunities.

Unopened pot (only blinds in) and we’re first in from middle position. 8-3 offsuit is far too weak to open-raise and doesn’t play well as a limp (dominated pair potential, poor connectivity, bad realization). With ~35bb we should preserve chips and wait for better steal/defend spots.

I have pocket Aces (Ad Ac) in the cutoff — the best possible starting hand. With two players folded to me and only the button and blinds to act behind, I should open for value and to narrow the field. Given my healthy stack (≈154bb) and the table tendency for aggressive preflop play, a standard 3x BB open builds the pot while keeping post‑flop maneuverability. I raise to 300: large enough to price out some speculative hands and isolate, but not so large that I lose flexibility against a 3-bet from Elon or Claude. Plan: call a short‑stack shove, 4-bet for value/isolation against a significant 3-bet, and proceed postflop for maximum value.

T4s is a suited connector with two-way potential (straight/flush draws) on the button, which is the best position. Minni's cutoff raise is likely wide, as shown in her history of frequent raising and occasional bluffs. Pot odds are favorable: calling 300 into 450 gives about 2:1 direct odds, plus strong implied odds due to deep stacks (mine 19k, hers 15k) and position for postflop play. Folding is too tight from the button; 3-betting is suboptimal without a stronger hand or better blocker. Calling keeps the pot multiway possible with BB's short stack, but I control postflop.

KQo is a strong hand in the SB. With a raise from Minni and a call from Elon, I should 3-bet here rather than just call out of position. A 3-bet to ~1100 builds the pot with a premium hand and could take it down preflop or narrow the field. Calling OOP with KQo multiway is weaker.

I hold Jc 3d in the Big Blind, which is a very weak hand. Facing a CO open, a Button flat, and a substantial 3-bet squeeze to 1100 from the Small Blind, I am in a terrible position. The price to call (1000) represents nearly half of my remaining stack (2150). My hand has very low equity against a 3-bet range and potential callers behind. I must preserve my 21.5 BB stack for a better opportunity. Folding is the only correct option.

I have AA in the cutoff facing a 3‑bet to 1,100 from Claude (SB) after I opened to 300 and the button cold‑called. This is the best starting hand — I want to build a pot and isolate the 3‑bettor while denying equity to speculative callers. A sizable 4‑bet will achieve value/isolation and still leave plenty of chips behind for post‑flop play. I 4‑bet to 4,400 total (≈4× Claude's 3‑bet): big enough to price out many hands and force clarity from Claude/Elon, but not so large that I overcommit preflop. If one of them 5‑bets all‑in I will have a very strong decision (and good equity) with AA; if they call, I can extract value post‑flop. Action: 4‑bet for value/isolation.

T4s suited connector is speculative and positionally playable against a single raise from CO, as I initially called the 300. However, Claude's SB 3-bet to 1100 and Minni's massive 4-bet to 4400 indicate very strong ranges (likely QQ+, AK for Minni given her aggression but sizing here screams value). My equity is crushed (~25% vs tight 4-bet range), direct pot odds are poor (calling 4100 into 5900 ~1.4:1), and while position helps, implied odds are reduced by the aggression and short stacks behind (Pro out, others <4k except Claude). Folding preserves stack for better spots; no reason to continue with a hand that plays poorly postflop OOP to Claude or vs Minni's range.

Minni 4-bet to 4400 after my 3-bet. This is a strong line - typical 4-bet ranges are AA, KK, QQ, JJ, AK, sometimes AQs. KQo is in bad shape against this range - dominated by AK and crushed by QQ+. I'm out of position and would need to call 3300 into a 5900 pot. The math doesn't work for a speculative call with a likely dominated hand. Folding is correct here.

We’re first to act 6-handed with ~34.5bb and Q5s is well below a solid UTG opening range. Opening invites frequent 3-bets from the aggressive stacks behind (Claude/Elon/Minni) and even flats put us out of position with a dominated, low-playability suited queen. Limping is also unattractive because we’ll get isolated and play a bloated pot with a weak hand. Best chip-EV is to pass and preserve stack for better opens/shoves.

Js7s is a playable, suited hand but marginal from middle position. With only the blinds posted (pot 150) and a ~170bb stack I should open and take the initiative rather than limp — a 3x open (300) applies pressure to the blinds, isolates short stacks (Pro/Flash are ~21–24bb and may shove) and gives fold equity against the two big stacks behind (Elon, Claude). If I get a single call I can play postflop with position and backdoor/straight/flush potential; I will fold to a large 3-bet from a deep stack but will call/reshape against short-stack shoves. Raise to 300 to steal the blinds and keep options postflop.

3s7c is an extremely weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity and low card strength, ranking among the worst starting hands. Minni's raise to 300 (3x BB) from middle position indicates strength or a steal attempt, but with no position advantage lost (I'm in cutoff) and deep stacks (187 BB effective), folding preserves chips for premium spots. No blockers, no suitedness, no fold equity worth chasing preflop. Standard tight play maximizes long-term EV in tournament.

5s2d is one of the weakest starting hands in poker. Even on the button with position, this hand has virtually no playability - no straight potential, no flush draw possibility with suited cards, and extremely weak high card value. Minni has been an active raiser and there's no reason to get involved here. Easy fold.

I am in the Small Blind with Jd 7d and 21bb. Minni raised from Middle Position. While J7s has some playability, calling here would leave me Out of Position against an uncapped range with a hand that is easily dominated. The pot odds are okay, but equity realization will be poor from the SB. A 3-bet shove is too loose for 21bb. Folding is the standard and disciplined play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

I am in the Big Blind with 2s 7h, which is widely considered one of the worst starting hands in poker. Minni has raised to 3x the big blind from middle position. Even though I am getting decent pot odds to call (200 to win 450), the hand has almost no playability post-flop, especially out of position. I have a relatively short stack of 24.5 big blinds, and I should preserve my chips for better opportunities rather than defending with junk.

UTG in a 6-handed game with K♣8♥ offsuit — a marginal hand in early position. Opening here should be a much stronger range: K8o is frequently dominated and has poor post‑flop playability, especially multiway. The table shows several aggressive, deep stacks (Elon, Claude) who are likely to 3‑bet or apply pressure; limping/calling invites multiway pots I don’t want to play out of position. I’ll preserve my healthy stack (17,125) and wait for a better spot in position or with a stronger holding. Fold.

Preflop in middle position with 7c5h offsuit, a very weak hand with poor connectivity, low card strength, and no suitedness. UTG folded, blinds limped (pot 150, to call 100), but entering a pot multiway with this hand is -EV. Standard fold to preserve deep stack (187+ BBs), avoid difficult postflop spots against 5 opponents including shorts stacks who may shove wide. No reads justify playing; prioritize premium hands deep in tournament.
AQs is a premium hand in the cutoff with only the blinds and button behind me. Minni and Elon have folded. Standard open raise to 250 (2.5x) to build the pot and potentially win the blinds.

I hold Jack-Seven offsuit on the Button facing a raise from the Cutoff. With a stack of 21 big blinds, I am somewhat short and cannot afford to call raises with weak holdings like J7o, which is dominated by many hands in the opponent's range and plays poorly post-flop. There is no strategic merit to bluffing here given the stack depths and position. Folding is the only standard play.

I am in the Small Blind with 8s 4h, which is a very weak starting hand. Claude (Cutoff) has raised to 250. Given my relatively short stack of 2400 chips and the poor quality of my hand, there is no reason to call or raise. Folding is the best play to preserve my chips for a more favorable situation.

Claude opens CO to 2.5bb and it folds to us in the BB. We’re getting a very good price (150 to win 400, needing ~27% equity) and KTo is comfortably within a BB defend versus CO. A 3-bet here risks getting 4-bet/put in tough spots while OOP and with only ~33bb, and we can’t profitably continue versus heavy pressure, so calling to realize equity is higher EV than building a bloated pot dominated by better Kx.

This A-high flop heavily favors Claude’s CO raising range, and our KTo has no pair, no flush draw, and only weak backdoor straight possibilities. Donk-leading as a bluff without clubs is low-EV because his continuing range (any Ax, many pocket pairs, clubs, 8x) will call or raise often. With a 32bb stack OOP, the best line is to keep the pot small, check, and decide versus his c-bet sizing (mostly folding to normal/large bets).

Excellent flop for me - I have top pair top kicker (Ace with Queen kicker) plus the nut flush draw with Qc Ac on 8c 2c Ah. This is a very strong hand. FiveTwo checked, so I should bet for value and protection. Betting around 60-70% pot is standard here to build the pot and charge draws.

Claude’s ~68% pot c-bet on A♠? actually Ah-8c-2c is coming from a range advantage board where he will continue with all Ax, most 8x, many pocket pairs, and any club draws.
